rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: practice(n) at line 24 ('The practice is yours now') is the AE101 M4 earned-landing carve-out — the engineered opener sentence the rule explicitly licenses at M4. Line 74 reuse is the licensed fluent-through-M5 use. No ritual/ceremony anywhere.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 voice quintet honored. M4 mood is sustained unease / curious-readiness so Risto-forward-optimism correctly does NOT lead; body is Boris-direct + Rory-reframe ('Past ten and you've become the agent'). No L&D-coach or motivational-poster register. No negation-callout self-contradiction; no atmospheric mis-tone.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rough-cut drafts for articles.
Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Huryn (three blocks), Klaassen (compound engineering), Uncle Bob, Laura Entis all credited inline. No training creator's name in body. No author-we in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses second person throughout; 'the student' appears only below the maintainer cut. No trainer delivery mechanics in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Un-packaged', 'gap analysis', 'three blocks', 'compound on evidence' all earn their landing in body (Key Concepts + Start here) tied to the move being run. No maintainer-only term-of-art leaks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 74:Nudging by hand is fine if you're watching. ... A handful of manual interventions is the practice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Doubled 'is fine' reassurance (line 70 'stopping the run is fine'; line 74 'Nudging by hand is fine') reads as the author defending pragmatism. The principle lines ('the trace is the result', 'a handful of manual interventions is the practice') already carry it — consider trusting them and cutting the 'is fine' framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Huryn named once in Key Concepts (line 36) plus the LO 'Name the three blocks'; Klaassen named once in body (Pre-reads, line 104). Each practitioner gets a single student-side cite; no credit-collecting.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Past ten and you've become the agent; call it' and 'the trace is the result' keep their punch; the surrounding lines supply the concrete boundary (a handful is fine, stop when you've seen enough). No over-broad slogan left without its boundary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'vendor' in body, no defensive 'not an aspirational X / this isn't marketing / the real practitioners' framing. Teaching beats carry substance, not positioning.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lead-ins and callouts at M4 stay at or below current vocabulary (send-off, walk, fill, gaps, un-packaged, trace). No LLM-internal vocabulary (token, embedding, inference, orchestration) planted ahead of its earning.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forcing functions live inside the fenced prompts; surrounding prose has no dangling demonstratives pointing at trimmed sentences and no bypass-the-agent 'do it yourself' punchlines. Section closers land on principle (line 74, 88).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No '## Debrief' / 'What happened' recap section — this module explicitly carries 'No Debrief' (line 43). The send-off IS the close; nothing to narrate in past tense.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Human-to-agent verbs stay peer-flat: 'Ask Claude', 'Give it your criteria', 'push back', 'nudge', 'correct a path'. No bark/order/command/whip/boss.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Frames forward ('The practice is yours now', 'the first long one'). No corrective 'you already think / you're missing' and no ability-tier pegging ('at your scale', 'for a beginner'). Pre-prompt gates (find transcript, pin SHA) are plausibly-failable for this audience.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Terms earn their landing (subagent audit, three blocks, un-packaged) or sit in signposting sections (What You'll Learn, Key Concepts) where the carve-out lets them name what the exercises land. Bare MN not used; full 'Module 4/5' forms used throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 file (software-engineer ICs), not the Agents 101 business-audience default. Tech vocabulary (branch, SHA, transcript, ADR) is the correct register for this audience.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'memory' used for the persistent walked-system artifact (CLAUDE.md + CLAUDE.local.md + memory + ADRs); 'context' as a noun does not appear in body (line 102 'context-window' is inside a URL). No 'your context compounds' conflation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body is calm forward narrative. Recovery branches (Connections blocker, audit passivity, send-off anxiety) all live below the maintainer cut as trainer push-back moves; no 'what if X fails' FAQ in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompts specify shape only where the teaching depends on it (protected task.md block, exact branch-name read-back); the send-off prompt is intentionally un-packaged with an output ask, not a templated structure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module. Compound-shape varies per module — never repeat same Debrief.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No Q1/Q2/Q3 retro. M4 uses the 'no-close' compound shape ('No Debrief... Compound on evidence, not anticipation', line 43) — the mood-demands-stew variant the rule explicitly names for M4-shaped modules.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M4 curious-readiness mood held; no M1 joyful-creation or M5 unleashed-leverage voice smuggled in. Send-off framed as experiment-not-production, consistent with the unease arc.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Read the agent's scrollback ... check the agent didn't just stall' (line 92) is a liveness check for M5 material, not manual error-catching of output quality; reading the trace is the emotional/diagnostic payoff, not a diff-for-bugs ask.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: In-person AE101 module file; no self-study simulation persona in scope.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Citations are one-line inline (Uncle Bob, Entis/Klaassen in Pre-reads). No consultancy-deck research callout box.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All forcing functions route through Claude (Ask Claude to locate transcript / commit / push / run the task). Git and power-settings happen via the agent or as ambient setup, not as out-of-conversation mechanic steps.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body line 17 names both layers explicitly together ('CLAUDE.md + CLAUDE.local.md'); the send-off prompt spells out 'CLAUDE.md team and CLAUDE.local.md personal'. Layer is disambiguated, not left bare.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Zero em-dashes in body. Sentences are short and declarative; commas and semicolons carry the few compound clauses.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Action sections lead with verbs: 'Start here', 'Send the task off', 'Bring to Module 5'. Canonical sections (Big Idea, What You'll Learn, Key Concepts, Next) keep their architecture-mandated names per the carve-out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All inline links use titles ('Test and learn', 'Will company memory emerge?', 'Reading the return', 'Claude Code for engineers, session transcripts', 'Clean Code Is Steering'). No filename-as-link-text, no code-span path links.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'memory' here is the walked-system component (observations / rules / ADRs), authored upstream at M1; no fresh first-authored write to a contested word is introduced in this module that needs a new disambiguation line.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Balanced and correctly slotted: 'the agent' (8x) for autonomous work, 'Claude'/'Claude Code' (9x) for invocation/addressee and product/UI surface, no thinking/drift beat requiring 'the LLM'. No assistant-shaped skew.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No tomorrow/overnight/this morning/tonight in body. Session-relative phrasing used instead ('while it runs', 'when the next slot lands', 'now'). 'prevent sleep on power' is a macOS setting name, not a time anchor.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No pedagogy-aware headers (Pause / Meta-moment / Step back) and no contemplative-pause sentence prefixes (sit with / let it land / breathe). Transitions use command verbs ('Before you send', 'Before you close').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompts have Claude act on the scoped task and report (commit, push, run, tell SHA), not ask the student to re-name a decision already made. No 'ask me which one' meta-loop in body or prompts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Text artifacts use .md (task.md, observations/). Non-.md identifiers are native formats (.jsonl transcript is Claude Code's runtime storage, not a student-authored notes file).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body detail is load-bearing for the next move (why pin the SHA, why local-by-default, why un-packaged). Implementation mechanics (jsonl path construction) sit inside the exempt prompt block, not in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Read the agent's scrollback ... check the agent didn't just stall' (line 92) carries a load-bearing follow-on (liveness check for M5 material), not a generic 'read it' after a chat-print.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forward closers keep the student as subject ('You pinned the starting point', 'Come to Module 5 ... you'll be scrambling'). No training-as-subject, no role-gating ('if you came in as a CTO'), no coach-imperative.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Module-specific operational setup (same-session send-off, laptop awake + plugged in, prevent sleep on power) restated at the moment of use in 'Send the task off' rather than left only in prework / training-architecture.
